phase1_kOutputMakeup.note: kOutputMakeup[2]=0.123 is already calibrated within 0.4 dB. ISS-008 is NOT a level/gain issue.

phase2_A1_spice_cross_check.key_finding: At BL=0.50 / 12.9 kHz, plugin wet ≈ −75 dBr at blend wiper while dry ≈ −8 dBr. Dry dominates by 67 dB — phase cancellation CANNOT explain the 54 dB discrepancy at these amplitude ratios. The pedal's dry MUST be similarly attenuated (~63 dB down) at 12.9 kHz, requiring ~55 dB of unmodelled dry-path HF rolloff.
phase2_A1_spice_cross_check.result: SPICE TARGETS MET — MAGNITUDE CORRECT, CANDIDATE SHIFTED TO (a)

phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.key_finding: Both V1L and V2 show large positive FR error at HF when BL<1.00 (flat dry mixed in). V1L starts with a too-quiet wet (−28.5 dB) so adding dry helps. V2 starts with a correct wet (−2.8 dB) so adding dry destroys the match. The mechanism is identical: the plugin's dry path is flat (full bandwidth), the pedal's is not.
phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.result: V1L SHOWS SAME MECHANISM — CONFIRMS FLAT DRY IS THE ROOT CAUSE
phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.v1l_swing: +35.4 dB from wet to partial blend
phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.v2_swing: +44.0 dB from wet to partial blend

diagnostics.root_cause: CANDIDATE (a) — unmodelled dry-path HF rolloff. The pedal's real dry path has significant HF attenuation (~55 dB at 12.9 kHz) that the netlist's 'IN_B → direct (no cap) → BLEND VR50.a' reading does not capture. Parasitic capacitance alone cannot explain 55 dB.

impact:
SYMPTOM (solid, reproduced two ways). V2's error is monotonic in BLEND while the wet path is correct:
  HF(5-13k) mean:  BL=1.00 +0.3dB (good) | BL=0.95 +8.7 | BL=0.90 +11.2 | BL=0.50 +15.3
  ab_report FR@:   BL=0.90 -> 8000:+13.2  12000:+27.1     BL=0.95 -> 8000:+10.4  12000:+24.4
  BL=0.50 top octave: 12.9kHz plugin -8.9dB vs pedal -63.3dB (delta +54.4); 16.3kHz delta +58.3.
NOT a noise-floor artifact — the pedal has real, deep HF rolloff there; the plugin passes the dry tap with only the tone stack's 7.2kHz R35||C32 pole.

*** ROOT CAUSE IS OPEN. MY FIRST HYPOTHESIS WAS WRONG — DO NOT RE-TRY IT. ***
I originally blamed kOutputMakeup[2]=0.123 (commented "still the placeholder") feeding kDryGain[2]=10.569, guessing the dry tap was ~12dB hot. REFUTED by ab_report's LEVEL/gain column: every V2 capture gain-matches to ~0dB (BL=1.00: -0.2/+1.4/+0.1; BL=0.95 -1.1; BL=0.90 -0.9; BL=0.50 -0.1). The broadband LEVEL is already CORRECT at every blend, so kOutputMakeup[2]/kDryGain[2] are not 12dB wrong.

Note also: kOutputMakeup CANNOT be fitted from gain-matched data at all — it is a global output scalar, exactly what the analysis gain-match removes. It is only visible in the raw LEVEL column (and only trustworthy for the identically-staged V1E+V2 set). Likewise OfflineRender's --out-makeup scales outGain ONLY; kDryGain is a hardcoded constant it does not move, so --out-makeup cannot probe the dry/wet ratio.

WHAT THE EVIDENCE NOW SAYS: level right + HF badly wrong = a FREQUENCY-DEPENDENT dry-path error, not a gain constant. Candidates, untested:
  (a) The real V2 dry path has HF rolloff we have not modelled (netlists.md V1 reads "IN_B -> direct (no cap) -> BLEND VR50.a" — full bandwidth; that read may be wrong or incomplete).
  (b) Dry/wet PHASE cancellation at HF that the model gets wrong. Suggestive: the pedal at BL=0.50 (-63.3dB @12.9k) is 6.7dB QUIETER than half its own BL=1.00 wet alone (-50.6 -6 = -56.6), which a pure crossfade cannot do — that is destructive interference, so the pedal's dry is NOT simply absent up there.
  (c) BLEND pot impedance loading modelled as too ideal a crossfade (cf. CLAUDE.md: real blend leaks the off-side, cap-impedance-limited, frequency-dependent, ~-22..-56dB asymmetric; and ISS-006's "NodalCircuit impedance loading" note on V1L).
  (d) NAM capture fidelity at HF (least likely — the wet path's HF captures fine at BL=1.00).

CASCADE (unchanged, still holds): blocks honest diagnosis of ISS-003 (V2 12.5k/16k, OPPOSITE sign so the excess MASKS it at BL<1.00) and ISS-005 (BASS hump; -8.4dB low-mid excess at BL=0.50). V1L does NOT show this, so it is V2-specific.

*** NO NEW CAPTURE-BASED ISOLATION IS POSSIBLE (confirmed 2026-07-16) ***
There is no BLEND=0 capture, and confirmed by checking every capture's full settings dict: no two captures in the 12-capture matrix share ALL non-blend knob settings, so there is also no algebraic way to isolate the dry path by differencing two existing captures. Additional untrusted captures exist but the user has flagged their values as unreliable AND they don't cover the missing knob-isolation combinations either. Do not propose "capture a BLEND=0 file" or "diff two same-setting captures" as next steps — neither is available. Resolve via (a)/(c) first — re-derive the BLEND network's true frequency-dependent transfer analytically/from circuit.md's documented cap-impedance-limited isolation figures — before any capture-based confirmation is possible.

Reproduce: python3.11 analysis/cascade_analysis.py (section C); python3.11 analysis/ab_report.py --filter V2 --os 8.

next_action.task: Examine V2 schematic crop schematics/crops/v2_TL_2x.png for any component (capacitor, resistor, filter network) between the input buffer output (U1B/U2B) and the BLEND pot's dry leg (VR50 pin 1). Also check if the dry tap is taken from a different point than IN_B.

resolution:
ROOT CAUSE: `kDryGain` — an unphysical per-path scalar that multiplied the dry/wet ratio by +20.5 dB on V2. Removed (src/dsp/Calibration.h + the 3 call sites in V1EarlyDSP.h/V1LateDSP.h/V2DSP.h).

THE BUG. `kDryGain[rev] = kInputRef/kOutputMakeup[rev]` multiplied the dry tap before it fed BLEND, on the reasoning that "the wet path has ~30-40 dB of circuit gain to absorb the output scaling, but the dry path is unity, so dry/BLEND<1.0 outputs are too quiet by 1/kOutputMakeup". That reasoning is false. kOutputMakeup is applied ONCE, GLOBALLY (PluginProcessor::outputGainFor:110), so it scales dry and wet EQUALLY and cannot skew their balance:
    dry_out = daw * G_dry * kOutputMakeup       wet_out = daw * G_wet * kOutputMakeup
The ratio G_dry/G_wet was already correct — it is set by the CIRCUIT, which is what the BLEND pot models. Scaling only the dry leg therefore multiplied that ratio by kDryGain: +9.5 dB (V1E), +8.1 dB (V1L), +20.5 dB (V2, worst because kOutputMakeup[2]=0.123 is smallest). The error was invisible at BLEND=1.00 (no dry in the mix) and grew monotonically as BLEND fell — exactly the reported symptom, and exactly why "level is correct, SHAPE is not" (the gain-match is set by the wet-dominated passband; the excess dry only surfaces at HF, where the cab-sim has rolled the wet away and the output is pure dry).

RESULTS (ab_report.py --os 8). Every partial-blend capture improved; every full-wet capture unchanged — the signature of a dry-leg-only error:
    V2 BL=0.90:  FR rms 10.15 -> 3.51 dB | 12k +27.1 -> +8.2 | 8k +13.2 -> +0.7 | null -6.1 -> -12.0
    V2 BL=0.95:  FR rms  8.22 -> 2.82 dB | 12k +24.4 -> +7.1 | 8k +10.4 -> +3.8 | null -11.3 -> -16.2
    V1L BL=0.65: null -9.6 -> -12.7   |   V1L BL=0.30: null -1.9 -> -4.1   (also fixes ISS-006)
    V2/V1E/V1L BL=1.00 (x5): unchanged within 0.1 dB. NULL gain columns stayed ~0 — level did not regress.
Independent corroboration from the §1 full-wet column (V2IntegrationTest): the boosted dry was leaking through the BLEND pot's cap-limited off-side even at BLEND=1.00, filling in the notch and propping up the LF edge. Removing it moved EVERY §1 feature toward its SPICE target — notch -21.9 -> -26.7 dB (target -36), LF edge +5.2 -> -4.4 dB (target -15; a POSITIVE LF edge was never physical).

TWO CANDIDATES REFUTED BY DIRECT EVIDENCE (do not re-try):
 (a) "The real V2 dry path has HF rolloff we have not modelled." REFUTED from the schematic itself (schematics/crops/v2_TL_2x.png): U1B pin 7 runs down and straight into BLEND VR50.a — no cap, no resistor, no filter. The netlist read ("IN_B -> direct (no cap) -> BLEND VR50.a") was correct. The line's only other branch is the out-of-scope J2 parallel-out.
 (d) "NAM capture fidelity at HF." REFUTED as a general claim by the V1L BL=0.30 control (70% dry, same bare-wire dry tap): it reads only -9.1 dB @12.9k — the NAM captures carry dry HF fine, and a bare-wire dry tap at partial blend behaves exactly as the schematic predicts. BUT see ISS-011: it IS the explanation for the one BL=0.50 file.

THE PREMISE WAS FALSE. This issue's headline evidence ("BL=0.50 12.9kHz plugin -8.9 vs pedal -63.3, delta +54.4"; "the pedal has real, deep HF rolloff there") rests ENTIRELY on the matrix's only `_2` take, which is CORRUPT — it carries LESS raw 8-16k energy (-49.7 dB) than the same revision's FULL-WET captures (-42.8..-46.8), which is impossible with 50% of a full-bandwidth dry signal in the mix. Filed as ISS-011. kDryGain had been fit to zero exactly that file's +16.8 dB level error (commit cef46ff, "V2 BL=0.50 NULL +16.8 -> -0.1 dB") — one bad capture fitted a constant that damaged five good ones.

GATE ARCHAEOLOGY (the durable lesson). Commit cef46ff, which introduced kDryGain, also WIDENED the gate that would have caught it: V2IntegrationTest's dry-path check went from `gainDb > -12.0 && gainDb < 12.0` (Phase 6.3's correct ±12 dB "near-unity" band) to `> 5.0 && < 40.0` — a 35 dB window that is not near-unity by any reading — because kDryGain pushed the dry path to +24.66 dB. Restored to ±12 dB; it now reads +4.18 dB, matching the circuit (unity buffer -> BLEND/LEVEL dividers -> U3B +10.1 dB -> MID -1 -> tone -1). Same pattern CLAUDE.md already warns about: a fit must never silently erase the gate that contradicts it.

ALSO CHANGED. V2IntegrationTest's `hf8k < notch` -> absolute `hf8k < -24.0`. The relative form coupled an HF assertion to the NOTCH DEPTH; when the notch correctly deepened past the (unchanged) HF, the gate failed on an improvement — it had only been passing because the notch was ALSO wrong. The real §1 gap at 8 kHz (-26.6 vs ~-40 dB target) is PRE-EXISTING (baseline -26.8), unrelated to this issue, and belongs to ISS-003; the new gate is documented as holding the line where the model is, to be tightened as that work lands.

VERIFICATION: 23/23 ctest green after a FULL `cmake --build build -j8` of all targets (the partial-build false-green trap in CLAUDE.md bit once during this session — a stale binary from a wrong working directory produced a fake "baseline"; both the baseline and the final run were redone with absolute paths).

FOLLOW-UPS FILED: ISS-011 (corrupt BL=0.50 capture — quarantine + re-render), ISS-012 (kOutputMakeup mis-anchored: it was fit to NAM-normalized absolute level, which is meaningless; with kDryGain gone, V2's dry path is left ~18 dB quiet for users — the fix is to anchor makeup on the circuit-derived dry-path gain, i.e. 1.0, which is provably shape-neutral). ISS-006 and ISS-003 updated (both were partially caused/masked by this bug).

New probes: analysis/iss008_dry_probe.py, analysis/iss008_rate_check.py.
title: V2 dry path passes far too much HF at BLEND<1.00 (+27 dB @12k) — level is correct, SHAPE is not
